scope: Two-stage turnout/abstention calibration plan for the Phase 2 mapper-backed backtest. Design only; no code or mapper implementation.

phase_2_3_evidence_summary.known_voter_conditional_dro_signal: strong (largest gap any year: 2016 R +2.24pp / D -2.33pp; abs-gap-sum < 5pp every year)
phase_2_3_evidence_summary.abstain_share_of_vep_signal: systematically biased; CCES post-wave weights scale to validated/registered voters not VEP. Survey reports ~0% abstain weight 2012-2024 vs 34.57-41.97% benchmark. 2008 close to benchmark (38.11% vs 38.44%) due to different weight construction.
phase_2_3_evidence_summary.unknown_share_signal: structural and large; 9.52% (2008) to 33.49% (2020); post-wave non-response; cannot be re-fit; must be carried explicitly or modeled.

Q1_two_stage_decomposition.rationale: Phase 2.3 evidence shows known-voter conditional DRO shares are within <3pp of FEC every year, while abstention shares require representing abstainers (which CCES post-wave weights do not). A monolithic joint fit would let strong vote-choice signal carry weak abstention fit, corrupting both.

stages.A: vote-choice fit conditional on observed voters; denom = D + R + Other survey weight; target = FEC D/R/Other % of total presidential vote
stages.B: abstention model + global calibration to benchmark VEP (count-level); operates on full sample but constrained at aggregate level not by sample weights

Q1_two_stage_decomposition.auditability: Stage A failure = mapper/vote-choice issue. Stage B failure = engagement-to-turnout function issue. Independently auditable.

A.name: year-level abstention prior from benchmark VEP only
A.reason: discards all individual engagement signal; renders mapper engagement field inert for participation; useless for subgroup analysis

B.name: subgroup/year calibration using survey observed abstention
B.summary: fit per-cell abstention rates from CCES Abstain rows
B.reason: CCES post-wave abstention is structurally underrepresented (~0% weight 2012-2024). Fitting on 0 propagates weight-frame bias into every subgroup. Survey nonresponse not MAR with respect to vote-choice; using it would cross-contaminate Stage A.

C.name: engagement-driven propensity, globally calibrated to benchmark abstention
C.summary: p_abstain ∝ f(engagement, demographics); single global multiplier α_year scales to match benchmark count
C.reason: preserves individual heterogeneity; aggregate match by construction; engagement has 'low' inherent uncertainty per A2 spec; subgroup breakdown emerges naturally

stage_b_specification_v0.propensity_function: f(engagement, demographics) = sigmoid(β_0 - β_eng × engagement + β_age × age_term + ...); initial calibration so f=1.0 at engagement=0 and f=0.05 at engagement=10; exact form is engine concern
stage_b_specification_v0.global_calibration: find α_year such that Σ_i (weight_i × α_year × f_i) = benchmark_abstain_count_year (one-dimensional root-finding per year)
stage_b_specification_v0.heterogeneity_preservation: global scaling shifts mean only; does not flatten distribution; respondents with engagement=8 vs engagement=2 retain different p_abstain after calibration
stage_b_specification_v0.fallback_to_option_a: if engagement coverage drops below 90% for a year, revert to flat per-year prior with explicit degraded_to_option_a flag

Q2_abstention_model.terminology_note: engagement is the mapper's continuous one-dimensional scalar (NOT activation); Stage B never re-binarizes it

Q3_unknown_handling.stage_a_scope: Unknown excluded from known-voter vote-share fit denominator (denom = D + R + Other only); inherits Phase 2.3 convention

sensitivity_analyses_labeled_never_silen.S1: redistribute Unknown to D/R/Other in proportion to within-state known-voter shares
sensitivity_analyses_labeled_never_silen.S2: redistribute Unknown to Abstain (all of it)
sensitivity_analyses_labeled_never_silen.S3: redistribute Unknown 50/50 between S1 distribution and Abstain

Q3_unknown_handling.rationale: Unknown is structurally informative, not noise. A respondent who took post-wave but refused to disclose vote is different evidence than one who didn't take post-wave. Future mapper revisions may use Unknown demographic profile to refine engagement coverage; bucket must remain accessible.
Q3_unknown_handling.ceiling: if total Unknown share exceeds 40% in any year, this plan needs revisiting (current ceiling: 33.49% in 2020)

stage_a_target_spec.predicted_pipeline: respondent → mapper PRISM signature → engine per-candidate distance + clearing-bar → implied vote choice = candidate at min distance
stage_a_target_spec.aggregate: weighted predicted D/R/Other shares of known-voter total
stage_a_target_spec.comparison: FEC D/R/Other % of total presidential vote (shares_of_total_*; FEC-PDF-verified)
stage_a_target_spec.report_format: predicted_D - benchmark_D in pp, per year and per candidate

Q4_first_runnable_target.why_first: isolates mapper + vote-formula from engagement/turnout function. Stage A failure = Stage B meaningless. Stage A success = Stage B becomes clean engagement-tuning problem.

stage_a_invariants.label: all 5 years produce a Stage A result
predicted shares sum to ~1: |pred_D + pred_R + pred_Other - 1| < 1e-6
all three buckets non-zero in years with full data (mapper does not collapse Other)
per-candidate gap within v0 acceptance band: |pred_X - bench_X| < 5pp for X in {D,R,Other}
stage_a_invariants.note: soft; failure surfaces labeled regression entry, does not abort smoke

stage_a_invariants.label: no Unknown rows leak into Stage A (sample restriction enforced before aggregation)
no mapper output uses pid7 / party-ID-derived fields as primary input to MAT or vote-choice node weights (per A2 spec §2.1 risk)

stage_b_invariants.label: aggregate calibration exact: |Σ_i (w_i × α_year × f_i) - benchmark_abstain_count| / benchmark_abstain_count < 1e-3
per-quintile abstention spread > 5pp (top-engagement quintile vs bottom-engagement quintile)
Stage B fallback flag set when engagement coverage < 90%

joint_invariants.label: Unknown handling tag carried in every output: 'explicit' (default) or labeled sensitivity
year-level total reconstruction: pred_D_count + pred_R_count + pred_Other_count + pred_Abstain_count + Unknown_count = total_weight (within tolerance)
benchmark loaded for every year; abort if any of D/R/Other/Total/VEP/Abstain fields null
no prediction language in loader or observed-aggregate module (Phase 2.1 + 2.3 modules remain 'what the survey says'; only Stage A/B paths use 'predicted')

explicitly_out_of_scope: mapper functional form for any specific PRISM target (owned by survey-to-prism-v0.md)
engine vote-formula (distance / clearing-bar / era-context multipliers; owned by src/historical/respondentVoteChoice.ts and era-activations system)
per-state or per-subgroup decomposition (national-aggregate only in 2.4; subgroups in Phase 3)
mapper revision rules (owned by survey-to-prism-v0.md §4 Mapper Revision Protocol)
EIG / selector / quiz behavior (out of Phase 2 boundary)

sequencing_proposed_not_committed.deliverable: minimal Stage A scaffold (mapperBackedAggregate.ts + smoke); placeholder uniform-prior mapper; verifies wiring end-to-end; smoke enforces invariants 1-7, 14, 15
first non-trivial mapper for strongest-coverage targets (MAT, ideological, religious, engagement); rerun Stage A; expect 5-10pp gaps
Stage B engagement-driven turnout calibration; smoke enforces 8-13
iterate mapper coverage on remaining targets; tighten Stage A acceptance band; introduce Unknown sensitivity analyses

open_questions_deferred_to_v1_or_phase_3: Is engagement-to-turnout monotone or U-shaped (low-information voters under specific mobilization)? v0 assumes monotone-decreasing.
Should Stage B condition on demographics in addition to engagement? v0 starts engagement-only for parsimony.
Is per-state Stage B calibration justified vs national-only α_year? v0 is national-only.
For Unknown sensitivity, are S1/S2/S3 the right three? Should an S4 (PRISM-signature-conditional redistribution) be added once mapper exists?
what_this_plan_does_not_do: no benchmark or candidate data changes
no browser / dist / output / live-data changes
